zh.lifeSummary: 當Julian Elizabeth Meals於1582年出生在英國賀特福爾阿波茲 明日香時，她的父親John Christopher Meals32歲，母親Elizabeth Webb32歲。她於1598年與Richard Edward Higgins在英國賀福爾萊奧明斯特結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1603年8月1日在她的家鄉享年21歲，埋葬在英國賀特福爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Julian Elizabeth Meals于1582年出生在英国賀特福爾阿波茲 明日香时，她的父亲John Christopher Meals32岁，母亲Elizabeth Webb32岁。她于1598年与Richard Edward Higgins在英国賀福爾萊奧明斯特结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1603年8月1日在她的家乡享年21岁，埋葬在英国賀特福爾。

nameMeanings.name: Meals
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Meale, with post-medieval excrescent -s. One origin of Meale is as a nickname from Middle English mele ‘meal, ground cereal’, perhaps for a maker or seller of meal. Compare Millman .

variant of Meale with plural -s. An alternative origin of Meale is as a topographic name from Middle English mele ‘sand-hill’ (Old Norse melr), as found in such placenames as North Meols, Ravensmeols (both Lancashire), and Great Meols (Cheshire).

variants.name: Millman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Meyer, Isadore, Golda, Iren.
English: occupational name for a worker at a mill, from Middle English mille, milne ‘mill’ + man ‘man’.
English: occupational name from Middle English mele ‘meal, ground cereal’ + man ‘man’, presumably either a grinder or vendor of meal.

nameMeanings.name: Julian
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the common Late Latin name Julianus, a derivative of Julius . In classical times Julianus was a name borne not only by various minor early saints, but also by the Roman emperor Julian ‘the Apostate’, who attempted to return the Roman Empire from institutionalized Christianity to paganism. For many centuries the English name Julian was borne by women as well as men, for example by the Blessed Julian of Norwich ( c.1342– after 1413 ). The differentiation in form between Julian and Gillian did not develop until the 16th century. Julian is still occasionally used as a girl's name. Notable bearers include the British classical guitarist Julian Bream ( b. 1933 ) and the British jazz pianist and bandleader Julian Joseph ( b. 1966 ).

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Julian Elizabeth Meals was born in 1582, in Abbots Langley, Hertfordshire, England, her father, John Christopher Meals, was 32 and her mother, Elizabeth Webb, was 32. She married Richard Edward Higgins in 1598, in Leominster, Herefordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 1 August 1603, in her hometown, at the age of 21, and was buried in Hertfordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Julian Elizabeth Meals est née en 1582, à Abbots Langley, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, son père, John Christopher Meals, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Webb, avait 32 ans. Elle a épousé Richard Edward Higgins en 1598, à Leominster, Herefordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 1 août 1603, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 21 ans, et a été enterrée à Hertfordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Julian Elizabeth Meals 1582 in Abbots Langley, Hertfordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Christopher Meals 32 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Webb 32. Sie hat 1598 in Leominster, Herefordshire, England Richard Edward Higgins geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 1. August 1603 im Alter von 21 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Hertfordshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Julian Elizabeth Meals è nata il nel 1582 nel Abbots Langley, Hertfordshire, England, sua padre, John Christopher Meals, aveva 32 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Webb, aveva 32 anni. Lei sposato Richard Edward Higgins nel 1598, nel Leominster, Herefordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 1 agosto 1603, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 21 anni, e fu sepolta a Hertfordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Julian Elizabeth Mealsが1582年にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアアボツ ラングリーで生まれた時，父John Christopher Mealsは32歳，母Elizabeth Webbは32歳であった。1598年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアレオミンスターでRichard Edward Higginsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1603年8月1日に故郷で21歳で死去し，イギリスハートフォードシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Julian Elizabeth Meals(이)가 1582년 영국 허트퍼드실르 애벗스 랭글리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Christopher Meals(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Webb(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 1598년 영국 허퍼드실르 리민스터에서 Richard Edward Higgins(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1603년 8월 1일 그녀의 고향에서 21세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 허트퍼드실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Julian Elizabeth Meals nasceu em 1582, em Abbots Langley, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Christopher Meals, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Webb, tinha 32 anos. Ela casou-se com Richard Edward Higgins em 1598, em Leominster, Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1 de agosto de 1603, em sua cidade natal, com 21 anos, e foi sepultada em Hertfordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Julian Elizabeth Meals родилась в 1582 г. в местности эббоц лангли, хиртфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени John Christopher Meals было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Webb, 32. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Richard Edward Higgins в 1598 г. в местности леоминстир, хирефордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 1 августа 1603 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 21 год и была похоронена в местности хиртфордшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Julian Elizabeth Meals nació en 1582, en Abbots Langley, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Christopher Meals, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Webb, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Richard Edward Higgins en 1598, en Leominster, Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 1 de agosto de 1603, en su pueblo, a la edad de 21 años, y fue sepultada en Hertfordshire, Inglaterra.
